session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8623 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by running the required commands to fetch and classify the PR feedback for PR #8623.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show file restructuring context between main and HEAD

session.tool_result:

"body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Verbatim source code copy detected.

Suggested fix: Replace with the source pointer format from `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md`:

```

session.assistant: Let me try a different approach for the git diff command:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C origin/main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C HEAD~5...HEAD

Suggested fix: Verify the correct file and line numbers where this pattern is actually implemented, or remove the specific line reference if the pattern was planned but not yet implemented." }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xncNq", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/ci/job-ordering-strategy.md", "line": 60, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Replace line number reference with name-based identifier to avoid drift when file changes", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific line numbers that will change.

Source: `.github/workflows/ci.yml:15-17`

Risk: Line numbers shift with every edit to `ci.yml`. A reader navigating to line 15 may find different content than expected, silently misleading them.

Suggested fix: Replace the line number reference with a name-based identifier. The `concurrency` block in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is the stable identifier:

```
<!-- Source: .github/workflows/ci.yml, concurrency block -->
See the `concurrency` block in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
